title: Pre-existing failing test: test_b0040_materialization_gate expects pending_materialization but gets event_floor
source: Surfaced during hackathon-pm 2026-05-29 full-suite runs; confirmed pre-existing (fails on clean baseline, not in coordination/execution commits).
why: tests/phase5/test_b0040_materialization_gate.py::test_run_parks_unmaterialized_custom_at_pending asserts status==pending_materialization, but phase5/run_proposal.py now runs the event-floor (B0048) check BEFORE the materialization gate, so an under-sampled custom proposal returns event_floor first. Likely the CODE ordering is correct (event-floor should falsify regardless of materialization) and the TEST is stale -- but the owner of the phase5 audit flow should confirm and update the test (or reorder the checks).
value_rationale: Low: one stale test; does not affect survival-book/execution/coordination work. But a red test in the suite should not linger.
scope: Confirm intended ordering of event-floor vs materialization-gate checks in phase5/run_proposal.py
Update the test to expect event_floor for an under-sampled unmaterialized custom (or reorder checks if materialization should precede).

history.reason: logged by hackathon-pm; pre-existing red test
chore(coordination): deliverable built (survival book + exec layer + Best-Tech + runbook); M2-M6/M8 done; M7 armed run CEO-gated; log B0087

Fixed by removing the leftover demo pipeline/primaries_phase5/phase5_custom.py (created by the B0040 materialization demo) -- it made the existence-only gate skip pending_materialization. Real materialized primary is cmf_meanrev.py.
feat(phase5): CMF survivors die in full M3 audit (9th null); DSR gate works; fix B0087
